sections.heading: 🌍 Global Market Briefing — Asian Session
sections.content: Asian markets are trading with cautious optimism following a mixed overnight session, as investors digest persistent inflationary pressures and geopolitical tensions in Eastern Europe. The Nikkei's impressive 2.80% surge is driving regional sentiment, while currency markets remain range-bound with USD maintaining moderate strength. Energy markets are responding to supply disruption concerns, pushing WTI above $97 handle.

sections.heading: 📊 FOREX — Currency Strength
sections.content:
1. CAD (+0.12%) — Oil rally supporting commodity currency
2. JPY (+0.08%) — Safe-haven flows amid regional tensions
3. CHF (+0.00%) — Neutral, consolidating recent gains
4. NZD (+0.03%) — Modest gains on risk-on sentiment
5. GBP (+0.01%) — Range-bound ahead of BoE speculation
6. EUR (-0.03%) — ECB dovish rhetoric weighing
7. AUD (-0.08%) — China growth concerns limiting upside

sections.heading: 💱 Key Currency Pairs
sections.content:
EUR/USD 1.1620: Bearish bias below 1.1650 resistance. ECB's cautious stance on rate cuts contrasts with Fed hawkishness. Target 1.1580 support on renewed selling pressure.
USD/JPY 159.08: Approaching intervention zone above 160.00. BoJ rhetoric intensifying but action remains limited. Bullish momentum intact despite volatility warnings.
GBP/USD 1.3431: Consolidating in tight range. UK inflation data tomorrow key catalyst. Neutral bias with upside capped at 1.3480.
USD/CAD 1.3789: Oil strength pressuring the pair. Break below 1.3750 targets 1.3720. Canadian employment data supporting CAD strength.

sections.heading: 💰 FX Flows & Positioning
sections.content:
• Corporate USD demand remains elevated in early Asian hours
• JPY intervention rhetoric increasing but speculative positioning unchanged
• AUD underperforming on China property sector concerns
• EUR longs being trimmed ahead of German PMI data

sections.heading: 📈 EQUITIES — Indices
sections.content: Nikkei leads with exceptional 2.80% gain on tech sector rotation and yen weakness benefiting exporters. Dow Jones strong at +0.55% supported by industrials, while S&P 500 adds modest 0.20%. European indices lag with DAX -0.53% and CAC 40 -0.39% on energy costs and ECB concerns. Asian session maintaining risk-on tone with technology and export sectors outperforming.

sections.heading: 🛢️ COMMODITIES
sections.content:
Gold $4529.10: Consolidating below $4550 resistance. Fed hawkish tone limiting upside. Support at $4500.
WTI Crude $97.70: Breaking higher on supply concerns. Targeting $100 psychological level.
Silver $76.84: Following gold's lead, range-bound between $76-$78.
Natural Gas $3.138: Seasonal weakness continuing, support at $3.10.

sections.heading: ₿ CRYPTO
sections.content:
BTC: $77532 (-0.02%) — Stable around psychological $77500 level. Key resistance $78500, support $76000. Institutional flows balanced.
ETH: $2129.96 (-0.08%) — ETH/BTC ratio 0.0275 showing relative weakness. Critical support $2100.
Top Movers: DOGE $0.1058 (+0.31%) leading gains. XRP $1.3690 (-0.23%) and SOL $86.87 (-0.35%) underperforming.

sections.heading: 📈 LONG
sections.content:
• WTI Crude above $97.50 — Supply disruption premium
• USD/CAD 1.3750 break — Oil strength continuation
• Nikkei futures — Tech momentum and yen weakness
• Gold $4520 dip — Fed pivot expectations
📉 SHORT
• EUR/USD 1.1640 rejection — ECB dovish bias
• AUD/USD below 0.7140 — China concerns
• Natural Gas $3.15 — Seasonal weakness
• DAX futures — European growth concerns

sections.heading: 📅 Today's Macro Drivers
sections.content: German Flash PMI (09:00 CET), US Existing Home Sales, crude oil inventories data. Monitor 10Y Treasury yields above 4.25% and ECB officials' speeches. Geopolitical developments in Eastern Europe remain key risk factor.
